key_insight: NOTES combines a DeepONet neural operator with CMA-ES to optimize PDE-constrained designs in a much smaller latent space, making inverse design more efficient and transferable.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a practical route to robust topology optimization for fields like nanophotonics and structural mechanics when direct high-dimensional search is too expensive.
method: Learn a topology-aware latent representation with a DeepONet-based neural operator
Run CMA-ES in the compact latent space instead of the full design space
Use the PDE solver only for physics evaluation, enabling transfer to unseen operating conditions
evidence_signal: In the abstract, NOTES reduces the design dimension from 256 to 25 and is reported to achieve over 95 percent efficiency for nanophotonic beam deflection, with structural designs reaching compliance down to 246.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the learned latent prior and the cost of training the neural operator, so transferability may weaken if the operating regime is far outside the training distribution.
paper_title: Neural Operator-enabled Topology-informed Evolutionary Strategy for PDE-Constrained Optimization
